internal_anchors.details: test.js Section 3 (Internal Links) passes. All href="#..." targets and showTab() calls resolve. Nav chain is linear and continuous: overview → evaluate → model → selftest → wins → pages → killshots → predictions → falsify → ai → refs. First tab prev=empty, last tab next=empty. All 11 tabs are reachable from the chain.

tab_structure.details: 11 tab-content divs with unique IDs. All showTab() targets resolve to real tabs. Active tab on load is 'overview'.

external_links.details: Sampled 12 non-citation external links via curl -I -L. 11 returned 200. https://www.noaa.gov/ returned 403 to curl (NOAA blocks scriptable user-agents at the root) but the site is reachable from browsers and the same domain's deeper paths (ncei.noaa.gov, etc.) all returned 200 — classifying as ok (bot-blocked, not broken). Citation/DOI links explicitly skipped per prompt.

mismatches.rendered: Of 69 WINs, 69 have been reviewed so far (1 remain in the audit queue). The findings below reflect the 69 reviewed WINs.
mismatches.problem: Internally contradictory: '69 of 69 reviewed' AND '1 remain in queue' cannot both be true.
mismatches.root_cause: build-scripts/generate-html.js line 588 computes CA_PENDING as `wins.length - reviewed.length`. wins.length includes the 058b sub-id tracking entry (total = 70 in wins.json), while TOTAL_WINS / CA_REVIEWED both filter to baseWins (3-digit IDs only, total = 69). 058b has code_analysis.reviewed = false, so the calculation yields 70 - 69 = 1 phantom pending item. The same bug also makes the 'Audit status: covers all 69 of 69 WINs (100% complete)' line on line 779 pass even though the earlier paragraph contradicts it.
mismatches.suggested_fix: Filter `wins` to baseWins inside the codeAnalysis IIFE in generate-html.js (~line 584), e.g.: `const reviewed = baseWins.filter(w => w.code_analysis && w.code_analysis.reviewed); pending: baseWins.length - reviewed.length`. Re-run build.

verified_matches.verdict_tallies: Refuted=9, Self-Contradicted=13, Misleading=23, Std Model Explains=17, Not Demonstrated=3, Unfalsifiable=4 — all match wins.json
verified_matches.code_analysis_counts: hardcoded=25, live_fetch=4, none=40, relabels_standard=49, post_hoc=67, derives_from_dome=2 — all match wins.json filtered by baseWins+reviewed

data_prose_consistency.details: All count placeholders resolve correctly except CA_PENDING, which is computed against the unfiltered wins array and produces the off-by-one contradiction above.

win_detail_consistency.details: All 70 wins.json entries (incl. 058b sub-id) have a matching id="winNNN" anchor in docs/index.html. Detail anchors in current build use the format `id="winNNN"` not `id="detail-NNN"` — the prompt's reference to detail-NNN is stale; the canonical anchor is winNNN.

win_number_alignment.details: Fetched dome site wins.html. Dome currently lists 69 confirmed WINs (WIN-001 through WIN-069). Our wins.json covers all 69 numbers. Spot-checked claim drift: WIN-001, 068, 069 titles match. No duplicate WIN numbers on dome site. Note: dome site explicitly marks WIN-025 ('2024 Eclipse 9-Station Confirmation') as [REMOVED] mid-document due to storm contamination, while our review still treats WIN-025 as live (verdict from wins.json). This may warrant analyst attention — either we should add a [DOME REMOVED] note, or our coverage of the original claim should be moved to a sub-id.

flagged.issue: Dome marks as [REMOVED]; our review still has it as a current claim.
flagged.suggested_action: Analyst review — confirm whether to retain, annotate, or restructure.

discoverability_infrastructure.details: docs/llms.txt exists, references the review URL and dome model by name, mentions '69 dome claims (V51.1)' which matches wins.json baseWins. docs/sitemap.xml is valid XML with two <loc> entries (main page + llms.txt). docs/robots.txt has Allow: / and Sitemap: directive. data/uncounted-failures.json has 5 entries, all valid FAIL-NNN format, no duplicates, all required fields present. docs/index.html <head> has 4 meta tags (description, og:title, og:description, ld+json) and one ClaimReview JSON-LD block.

expansion_tracker_continuity.details: ID continuity OK: EXP-001 through EXP-049, no gaps, 49 items. No phantom tracker entries (every status:complete entry's output_file resolves on disk). No stale pending items. However, monitor/analyst/expansions/ contains 19 *.json files not referenced by any tracker entry — the tracker uses suffixed filenames like 'EXP-004-sec31-32.json' but bare 'EXP-004.json' files persist alongside them, presumably from an older filename convention. These are not lost work (the suffixed counterparts ARE tracked), but they pollute the directory and could confuse the digest pipeline. Recommend cleanup.

workspace_only_files.details: 9 analyst files exist on the FUSE workspace mount but are NOT committed to git. These look like analyst work products from the WGS84/globe-fingerprint hunts. They will be silently lost if the workspace is recycled. Curmudgeon reviews and analyst expansions directories are in sync (all files committed). Recommend the workspace-sync agent commit these on its next run.

project_documentation.details: Spot-checked 7 file paths from CLAUDE.md file map (data/wins.json, data/sections.json, data/uncounted-failures.json, build-scripts/generate-html.js, build-scripts/generate-pdf.js, build.js, test.js, .github/workflows/ci.yml) — all exist. CLAUDE.md hardcoded numbers limited to the version history table (V4.0 '67 WINs', V6.1 '69 WINs', V4.9.7 '2017 tests') which document past releases and are appropriate. The ~70%/93% relabeling/post-hoc figures in the Repository Source Code Findings section are approximate descriptive prose, not live counts, and CLAUDE.md does provide the live query commands directly above them.

build_reproducibility.details: Cloned a fresh copy of the repo, ran `node build.js html`, and checked that the regenerated docs/index.html matched the committed file (git status -s clean). No build drift. test.js passes 2211/2211.

issues_found.description: Part 2b prose says 'Of 69 WINs, 69 have been reviewed so far (1 remain in the audit queue)' — internally contradictory. Caused by CA_PENDING using full wins array (incl. 058b sub-id) while TOTAL_WINS uses baseWins.
issues_found.suggested_fix: In generate-html.js codeAnalysis IIFE, replace `wins.filter(...)` and `wins.length` with `baseWins.filter(...)` and `baseWins.length`. Hoist baseWins above the counts object so it's in scope.

issues_found.description: 9 analyst output files exist in the FUSE workspace but are not committed to git, putting them at risk of silent loss when the workspace is recycled.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Run dome-workspace-sync agent or manually copy the 9 files to the clean clone and commit. Files appear to be from the WGS84 dependency audit and globe fingerprint work — non-trivial outputs worth preserving.

issues_found.description: Dome site marks WIN-025 ('2024 Eclipse 9-Station Confirmation') as [REMOVED] mid-document due to storm contamination, but our review still treats WIN-025 as a current claim.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Analyst should either annotate WIN-025 with a [DOME REMOVED] note in the detail text, or repurpose the slot if the dome reuses 025 for a different claim later. At minimum, the verdict text should acknowledge that the dome itself withdrew the claim.

issues_found.description: 19 orphaned EXP-NNN.json files in monitor/analyst/expansions/ — older filename convention (no suffix). The tracker references the suffixed counterparts; the bare files are residue.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Tinker can remove the orphans after verifying their suffixed counterparts contain the same or newer content. Not data loss — just cleanup.

summary: Build reproducibility passes (0 diff lines, 2211 tests pass). Internal anchor and tab nav structure are sound. WIN coverage matches the dome site (all 69 numbers). Discoverability infrastructure is healthy. Two notable findings: (1) MAJOR — a long-standing CA_PENDING off-by-one in generate-html.js that produces internally contradictory prose in Part 2b ('69 of 69 reviewed (1 remain)'); the 058b sub-id leaks into the pending count because the IIFE uses the unfiltered wins array. One-line fix in generate-html.js. (2) MAJOR — 9 analyst output files (WGS84 audit, globe dependency inventory, cascade analysis) exist on the workspace but were never committed to git; they are at risk of silent loss. Plus one moderate claim-drift item: the dome site has marked WIN-025 as [REMOVED] but our review still treats it as live. No build drift, no broken anchors, no broken external data source links.
